ground_truth.title: The Best Graphic Design Courses for Beginners in 2025
ground_truth.author: Sandra Boicheva
ground_truth.main_content:
Ready to dip your toes into graphic design? This guide has you covered with some of the best graphic design courses for beginners to get you started in 2025. And because I like to keep things fresh, I’ll be updating this list regularly when new must-try courses pop up—kind of like your favorite streaming playlist, but for design skills.

You’ll find full-fledged online certificates to quick, free and paid courses, and even practical tutorials that won’t cost a dime. The courses cover basic theory, working with popular graphic design software, basics of typography, web design, motion design, UI design and many other topics.

And stick around until the end, because I’ve also rounded up some of the best YouTube channels that offer free graphic design tutorials and courses as a little bonus.

BaselineHQ Graphic Design Bootcamp

Degrees/Certificates: No
Pricing: Free Forever

6 Parts Free Graphic Design Bootcamp

This is an awesome beginner-friendly course that teaches design basics without draining your wallet, and I absolutely recommend you start with this one. It was created by expert educators and it covers a broad range of design skills. Best of all, it stays accessible to anyone because it’s 100% free and will always stay that way. The course doesn’t give you a certificate, but it will give you everything you need to start your graphic design career.

What You’ll Learn:
Introduction to design (6 hours): existing skills, personal goals
Design principles (10 hours): visual, usability, and ethical principles
Tools, workflow, and collaboration (8 hours): design software basics, including Figma
Graphic design basics (15 hours): history of design, basics of typography, color, and layout.
Applied graphic design (30 hours): applying design principles to branding, editorial, and information design
Graphic design portfolio briefs (45 hours): designing three independent projects for your design portfolio.

Courses by The Graphic Design School

The Graphic Design School (TGDS) has been around since 2008 and was actually the first fully online graphic design school. Their courses are flexible, so you can fit your studies around your schedule. You’ll learn from tutors who know their stuff, they are experienced designers who’ve worked with big brands and bring tons of real-world experience. They also offer government-accredited courses if you’re after an official qualification in Australia.

Degrees/Certificates:
Yes (CUA40720 Certificate IV in Design)

Pricing:
Intensive Foundation Course: €3690 one-off or €325/month for 12 months (total €3900)
CUA40720 Certificate IV in Design: €4430 one-off or €377/month for 12 months (total €4524)
Design For Social Media: €420 one-off

Recommended courses:

Intensive Foundation Course

This course will help you start building real skills with expert guidance from experienced tutors who’ve worked with brands like Dove, Maybelline, and Virgin. The course is self-paced, so you’ve got up to 12 months to finish 11 modules (about 700 hours of learning). By the end, you’ll walk away with a Certificate of Completion and a portfolio.

What You’ll Learn:
The basics of graphic design
Typography fundamentals
Colour theory and creating color palettes
Advanced typography theory
The basics of photography 
Logotype design fundamentals
Design systems
Layout design

Web Design Course

Next, we have another self-paced course by The Graphic Design School, this time for web design fundamentals. You’ll cover the key principles of UI (user interface) and UX (user experience) design while learning how to create multi-page websites that work on desktops, tablets, and phones. The course takes around 200 hours to complete, and you have up to six months to finish it. Same as the graphic design course, you’ll get a Certificate of Completion and a portfolio.

What You’ll Learn:
Introduction to web design
Visual design elements in the web environment
Designing information and navigation
Scoping and project design specification
Project design prototyping
Publishing a design online

Graphic Design Courses for Beginners by Coursera

Coursera connects you with courses from top universities and big-name companies like Google, IBM, and Stanford. It’s a flexible platform with everything from short guided projects to full-on degrees. You can choose what fits your schedule and skill level, and since it’s self-paced, you’re in charge of how fast you move through the material.

Degrees/Certificates: Yes
Pricing:
Guided projects starting at $9.99
Courses are free to start (with optional paid certificates)
Specializations and professional certificates $49/month
MasterTrack certificates starting at $2,000
Degrees starting at $9,000

Recommended courses:

Graphic Design for Beginners

This course by David Underwood is part of the Effective Communication: Writing, Design, and Presentation Specialization. It’s a beginner-friendly course designed to help you improve your design skills, focusing on creating professional-looking PowerPoints, reports, resumes, and presentations. The course takes about 29 hours to finish, and you can work at your own pace. You’ll learn simple graphic design tips to make your work look polished and confident. The best part is, that you can use tools like PowerPoint, Google Slides, or Keynote to do all the assignments.

What You Will Learn:
The basics of good design, including key design vocabulary. (3 hours)
Grids, negative space, and margins, and how to choose the right design elements like type, color, and images. (8 hours)
Composition and visual design techniques like contrast, repetition, and tension. (10 hours)
How to take and apply constructive criticism, and the importance of revising your work. (7 hours)

Fundamentals of Graphic Design

This course is also part of the Graphic Design Specialization and is taught by Michael Worthington, who does a great job of breaking down design concepts for beginners. The course is about 15 hours long, and you can learn at your own pace. You’ll get practical exercises and projects to help you apply what you learn.

What You Will Learn:
Imagemaking fundamentals (3 hours)
Typography fundamentals (3 hours)
Shape and Color fundamentals (3 hours)
Composition fundamentals (4 hours)

Introduction to Typography

Anther Kiley is a typography expert who will guide you through the world of fonts and letterforms. It’s a beginner-level course, so no previous experience is needed, and you’ll learn at your own pace. The course is about 8 hours long, broken into four modules, with assignments to help you really dive into the subject. After completing the course, you’ll get a certificate. It’s not about learning software, but you will need access to something like Adobe InDesign to do the assignments.

What You Will Learn:
The fundamentals of typefaces, including terminology, measurement, and how letterforms shape the meaning of type.(2 hours)
How different typefaces tell stories and their historical evolution. (1 hour)
To manipulate letter spacing and use grid systems for effective typesetting. (1 hour)
How typography can change the meaning of text, and apply what you’ve learned to create a typographic poster. (3 hours)

User Interface Design: Principles, Prototyping, Practices

Karlis Zars and the Starweaver Instructor Team lead a course with a super straightforward and practical approach to UI design. It’s a one-hour course that’s great for beginners and after completing it, you’ll walk away with a solid foundation in UI design principles, prototyping, and how to design accessible and attractive interfaces.

What You Will Learn:
The basics of what makes a user interface easy to use and visually engaging.
How to create and test interactive prototypes.
How to design interfaces that are accessible, intuitive, and visually appealing.

Graphic Design Beginner-Level Courses by Upskillist

Upskillist is that friend who’s always learning something cool and bringing you along for the ride. If you’ve got a sudden urge to become a photographer, launch your own fashion brand, or brush up on project management, this platform has a course for you. It has over 80 courses, including ones that teach you graphic design and let you learn at your own pace, and earn a certificate.

Degrees/Certificates: Yes, courses offer globally recognized certificates from CPD.
Pricing: €59.99/month (includes access to all courses)

Recommended courses:

Graphic Design Course

This course gives you everything you need to start creating your own awesome designs. In about 16 weeks, you’ll follow 32 lessons with practical skills, all taught by experts who really know their stuff. And you’ll have a solid grip on Adobe tools like Photoshop, Illustrator, and InDesign, plus a diploma to show for it.

What You’ll Learn:
Elements and principles of design
An introduction to Adobe Illustrator and logo design
Flyer design using Adobe Illustrator
Photo manipulation in Adobe Photoshop
Movie poster design in Adobe Photoshop
An introduction to Adobe InDesign
Brochure design in InDesign
Intermediate in Graphic Design Module
Advanced in Graphic Design Module
Proficient in Graphic Design Module

UX Design Certificate Course Online

Upskillist also offers a full beginner to intermediate UX design course. It’s an 8-week program that has 16 lessons that teach theory with hands-on practice. The course is taught by experienced UX pros, and it dives deep into research, user flows, and prototyping with tools like Figma. When you’re done, you’ll earn a globally recognized diploma and a portfolio.

What You’ll Learn:
The core principles of UX design.
Top theories like Jakob Nielsen’s 10 Principles of UI.
Understanding UX research and how to analyze user needs.
Mastering user flows and creating app prototypes step by step using Figma.
Module 2: Intermediate level in UX Design

Udemy Beginner Graphic Design Courses

Udemy offers over 250,000 courses, including tons of options for beginners and advanced learners alike who wish to brush up on Photoshop, learn logo design, or develop typography skills. They run frequent sales, meaning you can often grab courses for $10 or less. Also, once you buy a course, it’s yours for life—so you can learn at your own pace.

Degrees/Certificates: Yes

Pricing:
Free graphic design courses
Individual Courses: Usually on sale starting at $10 or less
Personal Plan: Access 12,000+ courses starting at €10/month
Team Plan: €28/month per user (for small teams)
Enterprise Plan: Custom pricing (for larger organizations)

Recommended courses:

Introduction to Adobe Illustrator for Web Designers

Chris Richter created this course to help beginner web developers and online content creators add better graphics to their websites. You’ll get a simple introduction to Adobe Illustrator, learn how to export your designs as SVGs, and see how to embed them in HTML. The course includes two hours of video lessons, five downloadable resources, lifetime access, and a certificate when you finish.

What You’ll Learn:
Adobe Illustrator basics
Exporting Illustrator designs into HTML
Embedding graphics in HTML and enhancing them with CSS
Designing scalable graphics without complex CSS calculations
Working with SVG in Adobe Illustrator
Adding HTML-based graphics to web pages
Applying CSS classes to your SVG designs
Creating and embedding scalable visuals for mobile devices

Adobe Illustrator Tutorials: Beginner to Advanced Guide

This course is perfect if you want to build serious Adobe Illustrator skills from scratch within 3.5 hours of video lessons. It covers everything from basic tools and workspace setup to advanced features like scripts and automation. You’ll also get eight downloadable resources, lifetime access, and a certificate of completion.

What You’ll Learn:
How to navigate Adobe Illustrator’s interface and tools
How to create and edit vector shapes
Color theory basics and working with gradients, swatches, and fills
Tips for precise vector drawing using tools like the Pen and Curvature tool
How to work with typography, text effects, and custom text outlines
Advanced tricks to speed up your workflow with scripts and shortcuts
Using ChatGPT to create detailed line art designs
Adding filters, effects, and transformations to your artwork
How to export projects in different formats for web, print, or sharing
Real-world projects to practice and build your portfolio

Graphic Design Theory – Space

This course is taught by a pro designer who’s been in the game for over 20 years and has worked with big names like Disney, Lego, and even Adobe, where he trained top clients on design workflows. You’ll get 1.5 hours of video lessons, 4 articles, and downloadable resources, all with lifetime access. When you finish, you’ll also get a certificate.

What You’ll Learn:
How to layer elements to make your design feel less flat
Adding dimension with vanishing points and scale
Using negative space
Proximity and form
Texture and leading lines

Beginner Level Courses by Kadenze

Kadenze is a chill, easy-to-use learning platform with creative courses, including graphic design. Well-known universities and brands run the classes, so you learn from real pros. You can go at your own pace, connect with other students, and even get college credit for certain courses.

Degrees/Certificates: Yes
College Credit for Some Courses: Yes
Pricing: Some courses are free, but you’ll need to sign up for a premium plan for full access.

Recommended courses:

The Complete Typographer Part 1 and Part 2

This two-part course brings together some of the biggest names in design to help you master the art of typography. You’ll be learning from legends like Steven Heller, Angela Riechers, Tobias Frere-Jones, and Claudia de Almeida. Part 1 covers the history, theory, and basics of typography, laying a solid foundation. Part 2 builds on that with custom hand lettering, typeface selection, and even the business side of selling fonts. Oh, and yes, you can get a certificate in the end.

What You’ll Learn:
How typography evolved and why it matters
Typeface design basics
Practical ways to use type in different design projects
The tech behind type and how it’s used today
Understanding typefaces as more than just letters
How to mix and match typefaces in your designs
Custom hand lettering with Yomar Augusto
How to price, license, and protect your fonts

Introduction to Motion Design

This course is a perfect starting point for learning motion design. It’s taught by Allen Harrison, an experienced graphic designer and educator who has been working with big names like National Geographic and Oracle, so you know you’re learning from someone with real-world knowledge. The course covers how to use animation, timing, and transformation to communicate your ideas. You’ll get hands-on experience with Adobe After Effects and Photoshop.

What You’ll Learn:
How time-based media works in design
The basics of animation and timing
How to use Adobe After Effects and Photoshop for motion design
Simple techniques for transforming and animating elements

Graphic Design History and Methods

This two-part course is taught by Brockett Horne and Ellen Lupton, both experienced graphic design teachers at Maryland Institute College of Art (MICA). They’ll walk you through important aspects of graphic design, its history, and the methods designers use today. You’ll get a certificate once you finish.

What You’ll Learn:
Overview of graphic design history.
Key design movements like Art Nouveau, Constructivism, Dada, and Bauhaus
How fonts, posters, branding, and propaganda came to be
Short quizzes and exercises to help you apply what you’ve learned
The design process: storytelling, design thinking, and more
How authorship, ownership, and identity play into design
Multisensory design and understanding user behavior
Practical exercises and creative challenges to practice new methods

Free Graphic Design Courses for Beginners by Evanto Tuts +

Envato Tuts+ has free educational content for creatives with over 1,2K video courses and over 29K tutorials. The platform originally worked with a subscription model but its courses are for free.

Degrees/Certificates: No
Pricing: Free – full access to all courses and tutorials

Recommended courses:

How to design a logo in Illustrator 

In this course, Daniel White shows you how to design a logo using Adobe Illustrator. It’s a short, hands-on course where you’ll work through the logo design process, using a logo he created for a fictional coffee brand, Luke’s Iced Coffee, as an example. There’s no certificate, but you’ll get plenty of practical knowledge you can use right away.

What You’ll Learn:
The steps to take when designing a logo
How to understand and get started with a design brief
The foundations of creating a strong design
Picking the right fonts and using them in your logos
How to choose colors that work for your logo
A step-by-step guide to creating your logo in Illustrator
How to handle text in your logo designs

How to design social media templates in Figma

Adi Purdila teaches you how to design social media templates using Figma. You’ll get the hang of creating templates for Facebook posts, Instagram stories, and carousels. The course is hands-on, so you’ll be working on real-life projects while learning. It’s short but packed with helpful tips. No official certificate, but the skills you pick up will be solid and practical.

What You’ll Learn:
Why Figma works well for social media
Key design principles for designing templates that stand out
Getting the right sizes for each social platform
Walk through designing a FB post template
Learn how to create an Instagram story template
Work on creating a smooth carousel post

The Principles of Motion Design

Jess Riley walks you through the basics of motion design, breaking down graphic design and animation principles in an easy-to-understand way. The course is short but packed with helpful info, and you’ll even get downloadable project files to practice as you go. No certificate, but plenty of practical skills to pick up.

What You’ll Learn:
Creating flow and consistency with repetition
Basic animation principles for motion design
How to time animations just right
Making movement smooth with easing
How to build anticipation in animations
Adding follow-through to make animations feel more natural
Using secondary animation to bring designs to life
Styling techniques to make animations look great
Mastering the rules of design so you can break them confidently

Text Animation in After Effects | Motion Design Tutorial

Jess Riley breaks down how to animate text in After Effects step by step. The course dives into different techniques for creating cool text animations, and you’ll learn everything you need to get started, from basic movement to advanced effects. It’s short and sweet but packed with useful tips. You won’t get a certificate, but you will get a ton of skills to apply to your own projects.

What You’ll Learn:
How to create shapes from text in After Effects
Moving anchor points using the Pan Behind tool
How to work with null layers for control
Adding global movement to your animation
How to use easing and the graph editor
Timing your animation to feel natural
Creating loops for smooth animations
Animating text paths like a pro
Adding randomness and offset with keyframes
Creating shadow effects for depth

Graphic Design Basics

Laura Keung teaches you the basics of graphic design. The course covers all the essentials, like design theory, color, typography, and more. You’ll get practical insights and a strong foundation to help you start creating better designs.

What You’ll Learn:
The history of graphic design
Key design principles like balance and contrast
Basics of typography and how to pick the right fonts
How design theory works in real-world examples
Print design fundamentals
Digital product design basics
Brand design essentials
How to work with color and design assets
The role of technology and AI in design

The Basics of Print Design

Laura Keung’s course lasts around 1.2 hours, and while you won’t get a certificate, you’ll walk away knowing all the basics. You’ll compare Adobe InDesign and Affinity Publisher, so you can choose the software that works best for your projects.

What You’ll Learn:
Setting up your workspace for print design
Structuring your documents for print projects
Choosing a color palette for print designs
Typography tips to improve your designs
Importing images for print and getting them right
Creating impact in your designs
Preparing your files for printing
Exporting files correctly for print

Skillshare Graphic Design Courses for Beginners

Skillshare has hundreds of graphic design courses to help you start. There are courses on logo design and layout techniques, and you can also take part in a big community as you follow other creatives, participate in projects, and look at real-world examples. There’s also a free trial to get you started, so you can check out before committing.

Degrees/Certificates: No
Pricing:
Free Trial with just an email
Skillshare Membership: Price varies by region (login to see exact pricing)
Team Membership: $159 per user annually, with admin tools and team-focused features

Recommended courses:

Figma UI UX Design Essentials

Daniel Scott, an Adobe Certified Trainer, leads this course for beginners who are new to UX design and Figma. Dan walks you through every step of the way, starting from the basics, and keeps things simple and clear. The course doesn’t offer a certificate, but you’ll finish feeling confident and ready to design.

What You’ll Learn:
How to create and use UX personas
How to build wireframes
Proper ways to use colors and images in your designs
How to pick fonts for web and mobile apps
Designing your own UI components like buttons and icons
Understanding Components, Constraints, and Multi-Dimensional Variants
Speeding up your workflow with free UI kits and plugins
How to create a style guide for client handoff
Making simple and advanced micro-interactions, page transitions, and animations

Adobe Illustrator CC – Essentials Training

Dan Scott, an Adobe Certified Professional and MAX Master Award winner is your guide through this fun and practical course. This course is perfect if you’re just starting out with Adobe Illustrator and want to create beautiful designs. Dan makes learning easy and hands-on, so you can start designing icons, logos, and illustrations right away. The course doesn’t offer a certificate, but you’ll gain tons of skills to build your design portfolio and feel confident with Illustrator.

What You’ll Learn:
Drawing with shapes and lines, plus using the Shape Builder tool
Creating custom logos, advanced graphics, and mastering brushes
Working with typography, fonts, and vectorizing text
Using the pen, pencil, and curvature tools like a pro
Masking, warping, bending, and liquefying illustrations
Crafting repeating patterns and creating stencil-style images
Exporting for print, web, and social media

Logo Design: How to Create a Unique & Memorable Wordmark

This course is led by Khadija El Sharawy, a freelance graphic designer and storyteller who specializes in branding and packaging. Khadija walks you through her entire process for crafting wordmark logos that stand out. It’s a short and engaging class with practical advice.

What You’ll Learn:
How to understand a creative brief and break down a brand name
The importance of building a visual moodboard to reflect the brand concept
How to choose the right fonts and narrow them down for your logo
Manipulating fonts in Illustrator to create a unique wordmark
Picking a color palette that fits the brand’s story and vibe
Adding final touches to make your wordmark look professional

The Branding Masterclass for Graphic Designers: The Entire Process

Lindsay Marsh, who’s taught design to over 500,000 students, leads this detailed course on branding. It covers every part of the branding process, with brainstorming ideas to presenting a polished brand to clients. The course has practical lessons, templates, and a 50-page brand design guide to help you build stronger projects and portfolios.

What You’ll Learn:
Understand client briefs, ask the right questions and research competitors
Create customer personas and brainstorm ideas using word maps and associations
Sketch, refine, and finalize logo concepts using grids and design systems
Apply brand systems to real-world projects and create professional client presentations
Build Behance portfolio case studies and brand guideline manuals
Use Adobe Illustrator and Photoshop project templates for presentations and portfolios

Graphic Design Theory – Psychology of Design

Martin Perhiniak is an experienced designer, illustrator, and educator. His course will teach you how psychology plays into design, with 20 years of professional experience and over 100,000 students worldwide. He breaks down complex design ideas into fun, easy-to-understand lessons.

What You’ll Learn:
The universal laws of visual communication
Applying Gestalt principles like closure, continuity, similarity, and simplicity
Creating visuals that guide how viewers interpret information
Spotting design flaws and brilliance
Making faster, more confident design decisions using psychology

Graphic Design Courses at LinkedIn Learning

LinkedIn Learning is a solid choice if you want practical skills taught by people who know the industry well. It offers thousands of courses on everything from business and marketing to design and tech. You can also get certificates for completing courses, and they show up directly on your LinkedIn profile. It’s an easy way to learn and let potential employers know what you’ve been working on.

Degrees/Certificates:  Yes

Pricing:
Monthly subscription with access to 22,000+ courses
Free trial for one month

Recommended courses:

Learning Graphic Design: Things Every Designer Should Know

This course is taught by John McWade, a graphic designer who founded Before & After magazine. The lessons cover 1 hour and 5 minutes of quick, practical tips, and when you finish, you’ll get a certificate that can go on your LinkedIn profile or be saved as a PDF.

What You’ll Learn:
How to create a visual hierarchy
Tricks to make any layout look more polished
Ways to improve designs with simple adjustments
How to use spacing and alignment effectively
Tips for making text easier to read and more appealing
Common design mistakes and how to avoid them

Learning Graphic Design: Layouts

John McWade, a graphic designer with decades of experience, teaches this 1-hour and 2-minute beginner-friendly course. If you’ve ever struggled with making a layout feel balanced or engaging, this course breaks it down in a way that’s easy to follow. Once you finish, you’ll earn a certificate that you can display on your LinkedIn profile or download as a PDF.

What You’ll Learn:
How to design clean, balanced layouts
How to use white space effectively
Ways to create a structure using grids
How to combine images, text, and graphics seamlessly
Why visual flow matters and how to guide the viewer’s eye
Real-world layout techniques through two start-to-finish projects

Introduction to Graphic Design: Concepts

This course, led by Tony Harmer—a designer, illustrator, and educator—covers the basics of graphic design in a super approachable way. The course runs just over an hour (1h 3m) and is perfect if you’re starting from scratch or want a clearer picture of design fundamentals. You’ll pick up the key concepts used by designers and learn how to apply them to real projects.

What You’ll Learn:
How to navigate the four stages of the creative process
Key typography terms, type classifications, and basic measurements
How to create pleasing color combinations
Ways to use color to convey information and accommodate color vision deficiency (CVD)
How images, illustrations, and AI-generated visuals can enhance designs
Practical tips for creating effective graphic design layouts
Creative exercises to keep your design ideas fresh

Noble Desktop Graphic Design Beginner Courses

Noble Desktop has been teaching people design and coding skills since 1990. They offer practical classes where you can learn web design, UX/UI, motion graphics, graphic design, and even coding or data science. The courses focus on real-world skills, making it easier to land a job, switch careers, or build your portfolio.

Degrees/Certificates: Yes
Pricing: Prices vary depending on the course. 

Graphic Design Certificate by Noble Desktop

This course is for anyone who wants to become a professional graphic designer. You’ll learn directly from industry experts in small classes, either online or in NYC. The program runs for 84 hours and is split into five main modules, with hands-on projects that help you build a portfolio. You’ll also get extra mentoring, workbooks, and a digital certificate when you finish.

What You’ll Learn:
To work with Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, and InDesign.
How to retouch photos, create vector graphics, and design layouts for both print and digital use.
The basics of color theory, typography, composition, and design principles.
Creating balanced, professional designs that stand out.
Working on real-world projects like designing logos, packaging, and promotional materials.
Adding variety to your portfolio and showing potential employers your design skills.
Creating polished designs that meet professional standards.

Bonus: Free Design Courses and Tutorial on YouTube

Here are some of the best channels you can check out for detailed tutorials and full graphic design courses for beginners and intermediates.

Envato Tuts+

This channel covers a wide range of topics in design. You can learn graphic design, web design, illustration, and more. The tutorials are easy to follow and aimed at both beginners and more advanced learners.

Gareth David Studio

Gareth’s channel is great for learning graphic design, typography, and how to use Adobe software. He provides practical tips and tutorials that help you build real-world design skills.

Yes I’m a Designer

This channel belongs to Martin Perhiniak, an Adobe Certified Instructor. The videos are usually full tutorials on Adobe Creative Cloud apps, design techniques, and insights into the design industry.

Helen Bradley

Helen shares tutorials on graphic design, photography, and digital art. Her videos are helpful whether you’re just starting out or looking to sharpen your skills. She makes complex topics easy to understand.

Bring Your Own Laptop

This channel is a mix of design software tutorials and project-based lessons. You’ll get to work on actual design projects while learning the software and design techniques.

Graphic Design with Elena

Elena’s channel is full of tips on graphic design principles and software tutorials. If you’re looking to learn design concepts and update your skills, this is a great place to start.
with: Ready to dip your toes into graphic design? This guide has you covered with some of the best graphic design courses for beginners to get you started in 2025.
This is an awesome beginner-friendly course that teaches design basics without draining your wallet, and I absolutely recommend you start with this one.
Coursera connects you with courses from top universities and big-name companies like Google, IBM, and Stanford.
Here are some of the best channels you can check out for detailed tutorials and full graphic design courses for beginners and intermediates.

20 AI Tools for Designers that Are Actually Helpful
30 Super Handy Tools for Graphic Designers in 2025
Font Combinations: The Basic Rules and Best Practices
